mission.title: Install and Configure Cri O on Kubernetes
mission.description: CRI-O is an Open Container Initiative-based implementation of the Kubernetes Container Runtime Interface, providing a lightweight and efficient container runtime for Kubernetes. Installing CRI-O allows you to run containers in a Kubernetes environment with enhanced performance and compatibility.

steps.title: Add the CRI-O repository
steps.description:
Add the CRI-O Helm repository to your local Helm setup with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add cri-o https://charts.cri-o.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install CRI-O using Helm
steps.description:
Install CRI-O version 1.35.1 in the `kube-system` namespace:
```bash
helm install crio cri-o/cri-o --namespace kube-system --create-namespace --version v1.35.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the CRI-O pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for CRI-O
steps.description:
Edit the CRI-O deployment to set resource limits:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment crio -n kube-system
``` 
Add the following under `spec.template.spec.containers.resources`:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for CRI-O
steps.description:
Apply the necessary RBAC permissions for CRI-O:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cri-o/cri-o/main/examples/rbac.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the CRI-O Helm release with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall crio --namespace kube-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any CRDs associated with CRI-O:
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep cri-o | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the `kube-system` namespace if it is no longer needed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kube-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and state of CRI-O:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kube-system -o yaml > crio-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the CRI-O Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade CRI-O
upgrade.description:
Upgrade CRI-O to the latest version (v1.35.1) with:
```bash
helm upgrade crio cri-o/cri-o --namespace kube-system --version v1.35.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the CRI-O pods are running the new version:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are stuck in a Pending state, check the events for the namespace:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n kube-system
``` 
Ensure that there are sufficient resources available in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: CRI-O service not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the CRI-O service fails to start, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <crio-pod-name> -n kube-system
``` 
Look for errors related to configuration or resource limits.

troubleshooting.title: Networking issues with containers
troubleshooting.description:
If containers cannot communicate, verify the network configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o wide
``` 
Ensure that the correct network policies are applied.

troubleshooting.title: Container runtime errors
troubleshooting.description:
Check for runtime errors in the CRI-O logs:
```bash
journalctl -u crio
``` 
Look for specific error messages that indicate what might be wrong.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of CRI-O will have the CRI-O pods running in the `kube-system` namespace without any errors. You should be able to deploy and manage containers using Kubernetes with CRI-O as the container runtime.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add cri-o https://charts.cri-o.io
helm repo update
helm install crio cri-o/cri-o --namespace kube-system --create-namespace --version v1.35.1

prerequisites.description: Ensure that you have a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl tools installed.
